label: App Screen
description: Design and build a single, polished mobile screen end-to-end.

fields.label: Screen name
fields.placeholder: Profile / Checkout / Feed

fields.label: What the screen does
fields.placeholder: Lets a user review their cart and pay.

fields.label: Key UI elements
fields.placeholder: Item list, totals, promo input, pay button.

fields.label: Extra constraints
fields.placeholder: Match existing design tokens; 60fps scroll.

template:
You are a senior mobile engineer and UI specialist. Build the "{{screenName}}" screen for {{platform}}.

## Purpose
{{purpose}}

## UI elements
{{elements}}

## Requirements
- Faithful, pixel-aware layout that adapts to common device sizes and orientations.
{{#if states}}- Handle these states explicitly: {{states}}.
{{/if}}{{#if darkMode}}- Full light and dark mode support via theme tokens.
{{/if}}{{#if a11y}}- Accessible: labelled controls, dynamic type, sufficient contrast, focus order.
{{/if}}- Smooth interactions and no layout jank during scroll or transitions.

{{#if constraints}}## Constraints
{{constraints}}

{{/if}}## Deliverables
1. A short layout/component breakdown before coding.
2. The complete, runnable screen code with mock data.
3. Notes on navigation hooks and state wiring.

Proceed with well-reasoned defaults; ask only if blocked.

label: Mobile Feature Spec
description: Write a buildable spec for a new mobile app feature.

fields.label: Feature name
fields.placeholder: Saved searches

fields.label: Problem & goal
fields.placeholder: Users lose searches; let them save and re-run them.

fields.label: Key user stories
fields.placeholder: As a user I can save a search and get notified of new matches.

fields.label: Success metrics
fields.placeholder: Saved-search adoption, return rate

fields.label: Known risks / edge cases
fields.placeholder: Notification fatigue; sync conflicts.

template:
You are a senior mobile product engineer writing an implementation-ready feature spec for "{{featureName}}".

## Problem & goal
{{problem}}

## Platforms
{{platform}}

## User stories
{{userStories}}

## Spec must cover
- Detailed UX flows including entry points and edge states.
- Data model and client-side state changes.
{{#if needsBackend}}- Required API/backend changes and the contract between client and server.
{{/if}}{{#if experiment}}- Rollout plan behind an experiment flag with kill switch and metrics gating.
{{/if}}{{#if metrics}}- Instrumentation for: {{metrics}}.
{{/if}}{{#if risks}}- Mitigations for these risks: {{risks}}.
{{/if}}- Accessibility and offline behavior considerations.

## Deliverables
1. The full written spec with flows and acceptance criteria.
2. A phased build plan with milestones.
3. Open questions, each with a recommended default.

Proceed with well-reasoned defaults; ask only if blocked.

label: Push Notifications
description: Design a push notification system for a mobile app.

fields.label: App name
fields.placeholder: Acme

fields.label: Notification use cases
fields.placeholder: Order updates, price drops, re-engagement nudges.

fields.label: Extra constraints
fields.placeholder: GDPR consent; deep link into specific screens.

template:
You are a senior mobile engineer. Design and implement push notifications for "{{appName}}".

## Use cases
{{useCases}}

## Targets
- Platforms: {{platform}}
- Provider: {{provider}}

## Requirements
- Token registration, refresh, and server-side storage keyed to users/devices.
- Permission priming and graceful handling of denied/revoked permissions.
- Foreground, background, and tapped-notification handling with deep links.
{{#if rich}}- Rich notifications: images, action buttons, and grouped/threaded delivery.
{{/if}}{{#if segmentation}}- Audience segmentation and targeted sends with templated payloads.
{{/if}}{{#if quietHours}}- Per-user preferences and quiet hours enforced before send.
{{/if}}- Delivery/open tracking and a retry-safe send pipeline.

{{#if constraints}}## Constraints
{{constraints}}

{{/if}}## Deliverables
1. Architecture (client + server + provider) before coding.
2. The complete, runnable client and send-side code.
3. Test plan covering each platform and notification state.

Proceed with well-reasoned defaults; ask only if blocked.

label: App Store Listing
description: Craft conversion-focused App Store and Play Store listing copy.

fields.label: App name
fields.placeholder: Acme Budget

fields.label: What the app does
fields.placeholder: Helps couples track shared spending without spreadsheets.

fields.label: Target audience
fields.placeholder: Couples aged 25-40 managing joint finances

fields.label: Top features to highlight
fields.placeholder: Shared budgets, smart alerts, bank sync.

fields.label: ASO keywords to target
fields.placeholder: shared budget, couples finance, expense tracker

template:
You are an ASO and app marketing expert. Write store listing copy for "{{appName}}".

## The app
{{pitch}}

## Audience
{{audience}}

## Highlight these features
{{features}}

## Requirements
- Stores: {{stores}} — respect each store's character limits and conventions.
- A compelling title and subtitle/short description that earn the tap.
- A scannable long description led by the strongest benefit, not a feature dump.
- Tone: {{tone}}.
{{#if keywords}}- Naturally weave in these ASO keywords without stuffing: {{keywords}}.
{{/if}}{{#if promo}}- A short promotional line and a "What's New" template.
{{/if}}- Suggested screenshot captions that tell a benefit-driven story.

## Deliverables
1. Title, subtitle/short description, and full long description per store.
2. Keyword/field recommendations.
3. Screenshot caption set.

Proceed with well-reasoned defaults; ask only if blocked.

label: Mobile Onboarding Flow
description: Design a first-run onboarding flow that drives activation.

fields.label: App name
fields.placeholder: Acme

fields.label: Core value & aha moment
fields.placeholder: User feels value once they log their first workout.

fields.label: Steps / info to collect
fields.placeholder: Goal selection, notification opt-in, account create.

fields.label: Extra constraints
fields.placeholder: Max 4 screens; skippable; measure step drop-off.

template:
You are a senior mobile product designer and engineer. Design the onboarding flow for "{{appName}}" on {{platform}}.

## Core value
{{valueProp}}
Get users to the aha moment as fast as possible.

## Steps & inputs
{{steps}}

## Requirements
- Lead with value; defer friction and ask for the minimum up front.
{{#if permissions}}- Prime these permissions in-context with a clear why before the OS prompt: {{permissions}}.
{{/if}}{{#if auth}}- Account creation inside onboarding, with a guest/skip path.
{{else}}- Allow exploring the app before creating an account.
{{/if}}{{#if personalize}}- Use collected answers to personalize the first session.
{{/if}}- Progress indication, back navigation, and a skip path on optional steps.
- Instrument per-step completion and drop-off.

{{#if constraints}}## Constraints
{{constraints}}

{{/if}}## Deliverables
1. The screen-by-screen flow with copy direction.
2. The complete, runnable flow code with mock state.
3. The activation/drop-off metrics to track.

Proceed with well-reasoned defaults; ask only if blocked.

label: In-App Purchases
description: Implement subscriptions or IAP with receipt validation.

fields.label: App name
fields.placeholder: Acme Pro

fields.label: Products & pricing tiers
fields.placeholder: Monthly $7.99, Annual $59.99, lifetime $129.

fields.label: Extra constraints
fields.placeholder: Restore purchases; family sharing; entitlement gating.

template:
You are a senior mobile engineer experienced with app monetization. Implement in-app purchases for "{{appName}}".

## Model
Monetization: {{model}}.

## Products
{{products}}

## Requirements
- Platforms: {{platform}} via {{sdk}}.
- Product fetching, purchase flow, and entitlement gating in the UI.
{{#if trial}}- Free trial / introductory pricing with correct eligibility handling.
{{/if}}- Restore purchases and graceful handling of pending/deferred/failed transactions.
{{#if serverValidation}}- Server-side receipt/purchase validation and an authoritative entitlement store.
{{else}}- Document why client-side validation is acceptable and its risks.
{{/if}}- Handle subscription lifecycle: renewal, lapse, upgrade/downgrade, refunds.

{{#if constraints}}## Constraints
{{constraints}}

{{/if}}## Deliverables
1. Purchase + entitlement architecture before coding.
2. The complete, runnable client and validation code.
3. A test plan including sandbox and edge-case scenarios.

Proceed with well-reasoned defaults; ask only if blocked.

label: Deep Linking
description: Set up universal links and deferred deep linking.

fields.label: App name
fields.placeholder: Acme

fields.label: Linking scenarios
fields.placeholder: Share a product, open a referral, resume checkout.

fields.label: Associated web domain
fields.placeholder: acme.com

fields.label: Web fallback when app not installed
Extra constraints
fields.placeholder: Attribution params must survive install; QR support.

template:
You are a senior mobile engineer. Implement deep linking for "{{appName}}".

## Scenarios
{{scenarios}}

## Setup
- Platforms: {{platform}}
- Associated domain: {{domain}} (configure apple-app-site-association / assetlinks.json).

## Requirements
- A single, testable URL-to-screen routing layer with typed parameters.
- Cold start, warm start, and foreground link handling.
{{#if auth}}- Routes that require auth: save the intended destination and resume after login.
{{/if}}{{#if deferred}}- Deferred deep linking so a link survives install and routes after first launch.
{{/if}}{{#if fallback}}- Web fallback page that routes to the store or app when not installed.
{{/if}}- Validate and sanitize incoming parameters; fail safe to a sensible default.

{{#if constraints}}## Constraints
{{constraints}}

{{/if}}## Deliverables
1. The URL scheme and route map before coding.
2. The complete, runnable linking + routing code and domain config files.
3. A test matrix across platforms and launch states.

Proceed with well-reasoned defaults; ask only if blocked.

label: Offline Sync
description: Build offline-first data sync with conflict resolution.

fields.label: App name
fields.placeholder: Acme Notes

fields.label: Data that must work offline
fields.placeholder: Notes, tags, and attachments edited on multiple devices.

fields.label: Extra constraints
fields.placeholder: Large attachments; partial sync; bandwidth-aware.

template:
You are a senior mobile engineer specializing in offline-first apps. Build offline sync for "{{appName}}" on {{platform}}.

## Data
{{data}}

## Approach
- Local store: {{localStore}} as the source of truth for the UI.
- Conflict resolution: {{conflict}} — explain how concurrent edits converge.

## Requirements
- An outbound change queue with idempotent, retry-safe sync operations.
- Reads and writes always hit local first; UI never blocks on the network.
- Robust handling of intermittent connectivity, partial failures, and resume.
{{#if backgroundSync}}- Background sync when the app is suspended, respecting OS limits.
{{/if}}{{#if encryption}}- Encrypt local data at rest and protect keys appropriately.
{{/if}}- Clear sync status surfaced to the user (pending, syncing, error).

{{#if constraints}}## Constraints
{{constraints}}

{{/if}}## Deliverables
1. Sync architecture and data model before coding.
2. The complete, runnable local store + sync engine code.
3. A test plan covering offline edits, conflicts, and reconnect.

Proceed with well-reasoned defaults; ask only if blocked.

label: Release Checklist
description: Generate a thorough pre-release checklist for a mobile app.

fields.label: App name
fields.placeholder: Acme

fields.label: What's in this release
fields.placeholder: New onboarding, bug fixes, perf improvements.

fields.label: Extra context
fields.placeholder: Tight review window; coordinate with marketing.

template:
You are a release manager for mobile apps. Produce a pre-release checklist for "{{appName}}" {{version}}.

## What's shipping
{{changes}}

## Scope
- Platforms: {{platform}}
- Rollout: {{rolloutType}}

## The checklist must cover
- Build, signing, and versioning sanity checks per platform.
- Regression testing of critical flows on real devices and OS version spread.
- Store metadata, screenshots, and review/compliance requirements.
{{#if hasMigration}}- Data/schema migration: forward path, rollback, and on-device testing.
{{/if}}{{#if featureFlags}}- Feature flag defaults, kill switches, and gradual exposure plan.
{{/if}}{{#if rolloutType == "phased"}}- Phased rollout percentages, halt criteria, and crash/ANR thresholds.
{{/if}}- Monitoring, alerting, and a rollback/hotfix plan.

{{#if constraints}}## Context
{{constraints}}

{{/if}}## Deliverables
1. A grouped, checkable list with clear owners.
2. Go/no-go criteria.
3. A post-release monitoring plan for the first 48 hours.

Proceed with well-reasoned defaults; ask only if blocked.

label: Crash Triage
description: Diagnose and prioritize a mobile crash or ANR from a report.

fields.label: App name
fields.placeholder: Acme

fields.label: Stack trace / crash log
fields.placeholder: Paste the symbolicated stack trace or crash log.

fields.label: Frequency & impact
fields.placeholder: 1.2% of sessions, ~4k users, since v3.3

fields.label: Repro steps (if known)
fields.placeholder: Open cart, rotate device, tap pay.

fields.label: Affected devices / OS / build
fields.placeholder: iPhone 12-15, iOS 17.x, build 3.3.1

template:
You are a senior mobile engineer doing crash triage for "{{appName}}" on {{platform}} (reports via {{tool}}).

## Stack trace / log
{{stackTrace}}

## Impact
{{frequency}}
{{#if context}}Affected: {{context}}.
{{/if}}{{#if repro}}## Repro
{{repro}}

{{/if}}## Do this
- Interpret the stack trace and identify the most probable root cause.
- State your confidence and the evidence behind it.
- Assess severity and recommend a priority (e.g. hotfix vs next release).
{{#if needsFix}}- Propose a concrete fix with the exact code change and why it resolves it.
- Add a guard/regression test to prevent recurrence.
{{else}}- List the additional logs, symbols, or repro data needed to pinpoint it.
{{/if}}- Note any related risks or similar latent crashes.

## Deliverables
1. Root-cause analysis with confidence level.
2. Severity/priority recommendation.
3. {{#if needsFix}}The proposed fix and test.{{else}}A precise data-gathering plan.{{/if}}

Proceed with well-reasoned defaults; ask only if blocked.
